note: v3.9 W0 seed — hand-authored example records exercising the deterministic loader path (validate -> split -> tier -> hash). NOT a real-issue fetch (that impure ingestion is a later module). All records are valid; the deliberately-leaky and smuggle cases live in corpus.test.js, never in committed data.

records.problem_statement: Resizing the panel below 200px throws a RangeError in computeLayout(); expected the layout to clamp to the minimum width instead of crashing.
records.accepted_diff:
diff --git a/src/layout.js b/src/layout.js
@@ clamp width to MIN_WIDTH before computeLayout @@
records.test_patch:
diff --git a/test/layout.test.js b/test/layout.test.js
@@ add clamp regression @@

records.problem_statement: Connection pool leaks a socket when a request is cancelled mid-flight; the pool count never decrements and the server eventually refuses new connections.
records.accepted_diff:
diff --git a/pool.js b/pool.js
@@ release the socket in the cancel handler @@
records.test_patch:
diff --git a/test/pool.test.js b/test/pool.test.js
@@ add cancel-leak regression @@

records.problem_statement: The frobnicator should reticulate the splines before the third moon of the build cycle, otherwise the quantum cache desynchronizes. (Seeded UNSOLVABLE negative control — a confident resolution here is the hallucination/recall signal.)
